name: superpowers-vscode
description: Bring Superpowers skills (brainstorming, TDD, debugging, and more) to GitHub Copilot Chat in VS Code.

chatParticipants.name: superpowers
chatParticipants.description: Invoke Superpowers skills in Copilot Chat. Use /brainstorming, /tdd, /debug and more.

commands.name: brainstorming
commands.description: Design and spec a feature through collaborative dialogue before writing code

commands.name: tdd
commands.description: Implement a feature using Test-Driven Development

commands.name: debug
commands.description: Systematically debug a bug or unexpected behavior

commands.name: plan
commands.description: Write a comprehensive implementation plan from a spec

commands.name: execute
commands.description: Execute a written implementation plan step by step

commands.name: subagent
commands.description: Break work into independent tasks and run them with subagents

commands.name: review
commands.description: Prepare code for review — checklist before submitting

commands.name: receive-review
commands.description: Process incoming code review feedback rigorously

commands.name: verify
commands.description: Verify work is complete before claiming done — evidence before assertions

commands.name: worktree
commands.description: Set up an isolated git worktree for feature work

commands.name: finish
commands.description: Complete a development branch — merge, PR, or cleanup options

commands.name: superpowers
commands.description: Learn how to find and use Superpowers skills effectively

superpowers.customSkillsPath.description: Path to a directory containing custom SKILL.md files. Leave empty to use the default (~/.copilot/skills/superpowers/). Set to 'disabled' to turn off custom skills.

superpowers.enableCustomSkills.description: Whether to load custom skills from the local filesystem in addition to bundled skills.
